About Me

Who I Am

I am a first-year student majoring in Embedded Systems and IoT at the University of Science and Technology, The University of Danang (DUT). I am building my foundation in engineering and technology, with a strong interest in hardware systems and smart devices.

Through my Maze Runner Robot project I discovered how much I enjoy solving real, physical problems — diagnosing, iterating, and making something actually work. I am motivated to learn coding deeply in my second year and apply it to future projects. My goal is to become a skilled embedded and IoT engineer who creates practical, reliable technology solutions.

Strengths

Analytical Problem Solving
I break complex problems into parts, diagnose root causes, and work through them methodically rather than guessing.
Logical & Systems Thinking
I see how components connect within a whole system — useful for hardware where one small fault cascades through everything.
Resilience & Adaptability
I endure setbacks and adapt to changing conditions. When a plan fails, I adjust and keep moving rather than giving up.

Weaknesses

Over-reliance on Self-research
I tend to depend too heavily on researching alone when I hit a difficult bug, which slows me down.
What to improveProblem-solving speed and team interaction.
HowLimit solo research to 20 minutes, then proactively ask questions and discuss with teammates.
Public Speaking
I feel nervous in front of crowds and want to deliver more confidently and engagingly.
What to improveCrowd anxiety — reduce trembling and fear of judgement, control the body's stress response. Delivery — master pacing, volume, pauses, eye contact and gestures.
HowApply the "3-second rule" + deep breathing: stand still, breathe deeply from the belly for 3 seconds, scan the audience and smile before speaking. Structure every talk in 3 parts (striking opening → 3 core ideas → call-to-action closing) so I never lose my way.

Projects & Achievements

Experience

Academic Project · 2025
Maze Runner Robot
Autonomous maze-solving robot — design & build
  • Assembled and integrated ultrasonic sensors with L298N motor drivers.
  • Diagnosed and resolved hardware latency issues.

"This project was my first deep dive into autonomous systems. While I successfully handled the hardware assembly, I realised that my initial logic for maze-solving could be more efficient."

Ultrasonic Sensors L298N Driver Embedded Systems Hardware Debug
Personal Development Plan

Where I'm Headed

My purpose is to build a strong, well-rounded foundation — technical skill, language ability, and communication — so that I can grow into a capable embedded engineer. Each goal below has a clear objective, an action plan, and a measurable target I can check against.

Achieve IELTS 6.0 or higher
Jun 2026 – Sep 2026
Action Plan
  • Maintain at least 1.5 – 2 hours of self-study every day.
  • Solve Cambridge papers under real time pressure to get used to exam conditions.
  • Take 3 full mock tests: mid-term, end of term, and one week before the real exam.
Target / Result
Stable mock scores ≥ 6.0 in Listening / Reading (≈ 23–26 correct), with Speaking / Writing meeting the bar from an examiner or a reputable AI grader.
C/C++ Certified Associate Programmer
Jun 2026 – Jul 2027 · CLA / CPA — C++ Institute
Action Plan
  • Master advanced memory management (pointers, stack/heap) and object-oriented programming (OOP).
  • Practise code optimisation through bitwise operators and data structures (struct/union) applied in microcontrollers.
Target / Result
Earn the certificate with a score ≥ 85%, forming a solid base for Embedded Linux and RTOS in years 2–3.
